--recursive.help: Recursively search files in descendant directories.

--max-depth.help: Maximum depth to search directories (begin depth = 1 from/for each input path). Default maximum depth = 33.

--path.help: Source paths (directories or files) to find/read: Use ',' or ';' to separate paths; Extra separator ':' for Linux.

--read-paths.help: Input files list to read 1+ lines of source paths(like -p): Use ',' or ';' to separate; Extra separator ':' for Linux.

--skip-last-empty.help: Skip last empty line in each file.

--file-match.help: Regex pattern: File name with extension must match this.

--text-match.help: Regex pattern: Line text must match (Can use meanwhile: -t, -x, --nt, --nx, -e).

--has-text.help: Plain text: Lines must contain this text (Can use meanwhile: -t, -x, --nt, --nx, -e).

--dir-has.help: Regex pattern: Must has 1+ sub-folder-name of a directory matches this (skip inner files if not).

--read-once.help: Read once for link files (link folders must be or under input paths) which multiple paths link to one real path.

--ignore-case.help: Ignore case of matching/replacing for -t/-x/-e . You can add to one of them like: -it/-ix/-ie .

--enhance.help: Regex pattern to color output, inferior to: -t -x -o (skip -e if matched by them for a text).

--replace-to.help: Replace text from -x/-t XXX to -o XXX. If used both -x and -t: Use the closer one to '-o'; Or the left one if same.

--out-replaced.help: Just output replaced lines by -o xxx (just show changed files + lines which is helpful to preview changes).

--out-all.help: Output all lines including not matched; Or each whole block range if used -b and -Q.

--out-full-path.help: Output full paths if input relative paths by -p or -w. This can avoid duplicates and trim extra slashes and dots.

--no-any-info.help: Not output any info, no warnings no summary (if no errors), only pure result (Please always use -PAC or -PIC).

--no-extra.help: Not output extra info + warnings(like BOM); Hide each return info for -X; Output summary to stderr(for debug: -aPICc, -PICc).

--no-path-line.help: Not output path and line number at the head of each line. If used -X: Not show each command line before executing.

--no-summary.help: Not output summary info (at end). Use -A to hide when got errors. Use --not-warn-bom to hide summary for BOM.

--out-if-did.help: Output summary only if matched/replaced/found file/text; or execution(-X) return value = non-zero or unexpected(-V).

--no-color.help: No color for output (it's better to not add color if have subsequent matching or processing).

--time-format.help: Regex pattern to grep time/key for -B and -E : Use captured group[0] or [1] like: "(\d{4}-\d+-\d+\D\d+:\d+:\d+([\.,]\d+)?)"

--time-begin.help: Begin time/key as value of -F, like "2026-05-24 11:00:00". Just text comparison NOT by time value.

--time-end.help: End time/key as value of -F, like 2026-05-24T15:30. Just text comparison NOT by time value.

--sort-by.help: Regex pattern to sort result lines by captured group[1] if has, else by group[0]. If set to "" will try groups from -t .

--sort-as-number.help: If has used -s : Convert the captured group[1] (if has, else group[0]) of -s to number or decimal at first then sort by -s .

--list-count.help: Only output matched file path list or matched count.

--replace-file.help: Replace files, search text by -x/-t XXX , replace to -o XXX. Without this, just preview replacing.

--backup.help: Backup files if replaced files content (Rename them by appending last write times like: --bak-2026-05-24__08_14_52).

--single-line.help: Single line Regex mode to match/replace (Treat as one line for each file/pipe/block).

--replace-times.help: Maximum times to replace a line text with --replace-to. Use a big number or -1 to replace radically. Default = 1.

--up.help: Output [N] rows above the matched line by -t or/and found by -x.

--down.help: Output [N] rows below the matched line by -t or/and found by -x.

--head.help: Output top [N] rows of whole output if [N] > 0; Skip top [N] lines if [N] < 0; [N] = 0 will not output result rows.

--tail.help: Output bottom [N] rows of whole output if [N] > 0; Skip bottom [N] lines if [N] < 0; [N] = 0 will not output result rows.

--jump-out.help: Jump out (stop and exit) if has set -H [N] and already output [N] lines.

--row-begin.help: Begin row number of reading/matching/replacing each file or pipe.

--row-end.help: End row number. If input 0, end-row = 'begin-row'; If input '+[N]', end-row = 'begin-row + [N]'.

--start-block.help: Regex pattern of begin matching (skip lines above in files/pipe); If has -Q this will be the begin pattern for each block.

--stop-at-once.help: Regex pattern to stop reading/matching/replacing each file or pipe.

--stop-block.help: Regex pattern of a block end (must have set -b); Can set to "" if same with -b. Add -y if reuse it as next block begin.

--reuse-block-end.help: Reuse -Q (a matched block end) as -b (next block begin): For cases of blocks can only be separated from beginning(-b).

--execute-out-lines.help: Execute each final output line as a command. Will show command -> run -> show return value, if no: -P -I -A.

--stop-execute.help: Quit if an executed command return value matches Regex(like: "-\d+") or Math(like: ">=0", ge0, "!=0", ne0, eq-1, lt0, gt0).

--not-from-pipe.help: Force reading from files other than pipe (to avoid reading pipe if running in another command and no reading paths set).

--string.help: Input a string and read from it (without reading files or pipe). You can also use it to learn/test Regex, or test input args.

--show-count.help: Show matched count at the head of each output line.

--show-elapse.help: Show used time (in seconds) at the head of each output line.

--show-time.help: Show time at each output line head: dt,dtm,dto (s = second, m = millisecond, o = microsecond; d = date, z = zone, t = offset).

--show-command.help: Show command line and info. You can append text after -c for debug(like: -I -c xxx) for further extraction.

--help.help: See usage and examples below. More detail: https://github.com/qualiu/msr

--timeout.help: Maximum waiting seconds to stop and exit. No limit if value <= 0. Default = 0.000 s.

--no-check.help: Not check/fix source paths. Case like reading(-w) source paths list file from 'git ls-files'.

--nx.help: Exclude/Skip rows by plain text: Line text must not include this text.

--nt.help: Regex pattern: Exclude/Skip rows if line matches this regex.

--nf.help: Regex pattern: Exclude/Skip files by file name (including extension).

--pp.help: Regex pattern: Must match this regex for full path of files. Support '/' on Windows.

--np.help: Regex pattern: Exclude/Skip files if full path matches this. Support '/' on Windows.

--nd.help: Regex pattern: Exclude/Skip directories if find 1 sub-folder-name matches (skip inner files too).

--xp.help: Plain text list: Exclude/Skip files if has 1+ text in file path. Use ',' or ';' to separate. Support '/' on Windows.

--sp.help: Plain text list: Must find all texts in file path. Use ',' or ';' to separate. Support '/' on Windows.

--xd.help: Exclude/Skip link directories/folders (skip inner files too).

--colors.help: Set fore_back colors for -t/-e/-x;d/f/p;m/u like: 'Red' or 't=Red,x=Yellow,e=Green' or 't = red + Yellow_Blue, x = Cyan'.

--keep-color.help: Keep color of output result for Windows/MinGW - to be uniform color style with Cygwin/Linux/MacOS.

--unix-slash.help: Set 1 to output uniform forward slash '/' on Windows + MinGW + Cygwin, like 'c:/' instead of '/c/' or '/cygdrive/c/'.

--to-stderr.help: Output result to stderr. Default: result -> stdout, error/warn/info/verbose -> stderr.

--dsc.help: Descending order for sorting of matching (-t/-x), list(-l with --wt --sz), sorting-key (-s), time (-F with -B -E), etc.

--wt.help: Sort file list by last write time (with -l). If used both --wt and --sz, order by prior then by latter.

--w1.help: File time begin, file or time or ago(d/h/m/s): 'add_1s_if_no_w2.md' or '2026-05-24T18:30' or '3h'='-3h' = 'now - 3hours'.

--w2.help: File time end, file or time or ago(d/h/m/s): 'range2.exe' or '18:30:01' or '1d'='+1d' = 'begin + 1day', 'now-1d' = 'now - 1day'.

--sz.help: Sort file list by file size (with -l) and display with a unit like: B,KB,MB,GB,TB,PB,EB etc.

--s1.help: Lower bound of file size, format like 100kb (No space between number and unit, use B if no unit).

--s2.help: Upper bound of file size, format like 2.5M (No space between number and unit, use B if no unit).

--force.help: Force replacing BOM files. Default: only replace files of ANSI + UTF-8 no BOM + BOM header bytes = 0xEFBBBF.

--not-warn-bom.help: Not output BOM warnings when reading BOM files which BOM header bytes != 0xEFBBBF.

--block.help: Output specific matched blocks by block numbers(start from 1 of output), like: 1 or 1,2,3 or 1~3.

--sep-block.help: Output [N] empty lines between blocks as a separator. [N] = empty line count.

--out-index.help: Output index(column) of first match for each row text. If no -P, output will be: File-Path:{row}:{column}: {line-text}

--exit.help: Change return value, format: [Number] or [Regex-or-Math]-to-[Exit-Code], like: '1' or '-?\d+-to-1' or 'lt0-to-1,255-to-1'.

--verbose.help: Show parsed arguments, return value, time zone and EXE path, content error rows, BOM info, link files' real paths, etc.
